summary: Crystal‑clear FOB audience recording captures a powerhouse 11/2/85 show, highlighted by an explosive Estimated Prophet, blistering China Doll and a transcendent Morning Dew.
review: The consensus among reviewers is that this tape offers superior sound – full‑frequency response, clear instrument separation and virtually no hiss – making it the best source for the night. Musically the concert is described as one of the heaviest “DEWs” of the mid‑80s, with Jerry Garcia in peak distortion mode, a ferocious China Doll, soaring Estimated Prophet into Uncle John’s Band and an unforgettable Morning Dew that many cite as the show’s emotional climax.

ai_rating.rationale: 14 reviews (13 five‑star, 1 four‑star) uniformly praise both performance and tape quality; the weighted average yields a near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly note superior spectrum response, crystal‑clear mix, full‑range instrument definition and a cleaner capture than other tapes from the same date. No significant hiss or drop‑outs were reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows naturally from a tight Estimated Prophet jam into Uncle John’s Band, then launches into an intense China Doll that segues into a powerful drum showcase and culminates in a soaring Morning Dew. Reviewers highlight the seamless transitions and sustained energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as ‘cranking up distortion’, delivering his deepest, most ferocious playing of the era – a true high‑point of the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass bomb in Morning Dew receives special praise; reviewers note his presence and contribution to the song’s emotional weight.
band_member_comments.Drums: The post‑China Doll drum segment (‘drumz’) is highlighted as a musical high point, with fans calling it powerful and well‑captured.
